steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion — 'much like the filters in 3D glasses once worn at movies.' Look at what it does in context: it follows a description of the experimental setup (color filters on mantis faces) and draws a parallel to something familiar.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It emphasizes a difference between the research on praying mantis vision and research on other animals' vision.: The phrase 'much like' signals similarity, not difference. ✗
  • Choice B — It identifies a potential problem that the researchers faced while studying the praying mantises.: No problem is mentioned; the comparison is explanatory, not diagnostic. ✗
  • Choice C — It offers a comparison meant to aid understanding of the praying mantis study.: 'Much like' explicitly compares the color-filter method to familiar 3D movie glasses, making the experimental setup easier to picture. ✓
  • Choice D — It describes an earlier use of a tool the researchers used in the praying mantis study.: The underlined portion never says the researchers used 3D glasses; it only draws a likeness to them. ✗
That is the correct answer.
